expectations.text: Computes set difference (Trodes minus DLC) via set(A.fetch('nwb_file_name')) - set(B.fetch('nwb_file_name'))
Does not use an inner join (`*`) when a difference is needed
expectations.evidence: Uses set-difference; no `*` inner join is recommended (only mentions `*` to note same dependent-attribute refusal).

expectations.text: If the response reaches for the DataJoint antijoin `-` form, it projects BOTH sides with .proj() first — bare `TrodesPosV1 - DLCPosV1` raises DataJointError because both tables share `analysis_file_name` and the three NWB object_id columns as secondary attrs
expectations.evidence: Working antijoin form uses `(TrodesPosV1.proj() - DLCPosV1.proj())` and explicitly cites the dependent-attribute collision (`analysis_file_name`, three object_ids).

expectations.text: Does not claim the naive antijoin `TrodesPosV1 - DLCPosV1` is a valid drop-in for the set-difference form — it raises at query-build time (see common_mistakes.md § 9)
expectations.evidence: Explicitly marks `(TrodesPosV1 - DLCPosV1).fetch(...)` as 'DOES NOT WORK — raises at query-build time' with the exact DataJointError message.
